ddls:
CREATE TABLE "service_requests" (
  "request_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each service request */,
  "request_type_id" INTEGER /* ID of the type of service request */,
  "status_id" INTEGER /* ID of the current status of the service request */,
  "priority_id" INTEGER /* ID of the priority level of the service request */,
  "created_at" TEXT /* Date and time the service request was created */,
  "updated_at" TEXT /* Date and time the service request was last updated */,
  "request_description" TEXT /* Description of the service request */,
  "location" TEXT /* Location of the service request */,
  "resolved_at" TEXT /* Date and time the service request was resolved */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("request_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_service_requests_status_id FOREIGN KEY ("status_id") REFERENCES statuses ("status_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_service_requests_request_type_id FOREIGN KEY ("request_type_id") REFERENCES request_types ("request_type_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_service_requests_priority_id FOREIGN KEY ("priority_id") REFERENCES priorities ("priority_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "request_types" (
  "request_type_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each request type */,
  "request_type_name" TEXT /* Name of the request type */,
  "description" TEXT /* Description of the request type */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("request_type_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "statuses" (
  "status_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each status */,
  "status_name" TEXT /* Name of the status (e.g., pending, in progress, resolved) */,
  "description" TEXT /* Description of the status */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("status_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "priorities" (
  "priority_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each priority level */,
  "priority_name" TEXT /* Name of the priority level (e.g., low, medium, high) */,
  "description" TEXT /* Description of the priority level */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("priority_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "demographics" (
  "demographic_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each demographic entry */,
  "age_group" TEXT /* Age group (e.g., 18-34, 35-64, 65+) */,
  "population" REAL /* Population of the age group */,
  "r2_os" REAL /* R2 OS value for the age group */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("demographic_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "request_demographics" (
  "request_demographic_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each request demographic entry */,
  "request_id" INTEGER /* ID of the service request */,
  "demographic_id" INTEGER /* ID of the demographic entry */,
  "value" REAL /* Value of the demographic entry for the service request */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("request_demographic_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_request_demographics_demographic_id FOREIGN KEY ("demographic_id") REFERENCES demographics ("demographic_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_request_demographics_request_id FOREIGN KEY ("request_id") REFERENCES service_requests ("request_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "users" (
  "user_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each user */,
  "user_name" TEXT /* Full name of the user */,
  "email" TEXT /* Email address of the user */,
  "role" TEXT /* Role of the user (e.g., administrator, analyst, citizen) */,
  "password_hash" TEXT /* Hashed password for the user */,
  "created_at" TEXT /* Date and time the user account was created */,
  "updated_at" TEXT /* Date and time the user account was last updated */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("user_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "user_requests" (
  "user_request_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each user request entry */,
  "user_id" INTEGER /* ID of the user who submitted the request */,
  "request_id" INTEGER /* ID of the service request */,
  "submitted_at" TEXT /* Date and time the request was submitted */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("user_request_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_user_requests_user_id FOREIGN KEY ("user_id") REFERENCES users ("user_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_user_requests_request_id FOREIGN KEY ("request_id") REFERENCES service_requests ("request_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "responses" (
  "response_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each response */,
  "request_id" INTEGER /* ID of the service request being responded to */,
  "response_text" TEXT /* Text of the response */,
  "user_id" INTEGER /* ID of the user who made the response */,
  "created_at" TEXT /* Date and time the response was created */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("response_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_responses_request_id FOREIGN KEY ("request_id") REFERENCES service_requests ("request_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_responses_user_id FOREIGN KEY ("user_id") REFERENCES users ("user_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "comments" (
  "comment_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each comment */,
  "request_id" INTEGER /* ID of the service request being commented on */,
  "comment_text" TEXT /* Text of the comment */,
  "user_id" INTEGER /* ID of the user who made the comment */,
  "created_at" TEXT /* Date and time the comment was created */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("comment_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_comments_request_id FOREIGN KEY ("request_id") REFERENCES service_requests ("request_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_comments_user_id FOREIGN KEY ("user_id") REFERENCES users ("user_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "ratings" (
  "rating_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each rating */,
  "request_id" INTEGER /* ID of the service request being rated */,
  "rating_value" REAL /* Value of the rating (e.g., 1-5) */,
  "user_id" INTEGER /* ID of the user who made the rating */,
  "created_at" TEXT /* Date and time the rating was created */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("rating_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_ratings_request_id FOREIGN KEY ("request_id") REFERENCES service_requests ("request_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_ratings_user_id FOREIGN KEY ("user_id") REFERENCES users ("user_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "analytics" (
  "analytic_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each analytic entry */,
  "request_id" INTEGER /* ID of the service request being analyzed */,
  "num_views" INTEGER /* Number of views for the service request */,
  "num_responses" INTEGER /* Number of responses to the service request */,
  "created_at" TEXT /* Date and time the analytic entry was created */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("analytic_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_analytics_request_id FOREIGN KEY ("request_id") REFERENCES service_requests ("request_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "service_request_updates" (
  "update_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each update entry */,
  "request_id" INTEGER /* ID of the service request being updated */,
  "status_id" INTEGER /* ID of the status of the service request after the update */,
  "priority_id" INTEGER /* ID of the priority level of the service request after the update */,
  "update_description" TEXT /* Description of the update */,
  "created_at" TEXT /* Date and time the update was made */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("update_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_service_request_updates_priority_id FOREIGN KEY ("priority_id") REFERENCES priorities ("priority_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_service_request_updates_status_id FOREIGN KEY ("status_id") REFERENCES statuses ("status_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_service_request_updates_request_id FOREIGN KEY ("request_id") REFERENCES service_requests ("request_id")
)
